summary: SetPumpSpeed drops the bogus Speed parameter and sends IsOn as dataType=int (0-100) matching Hayward's overloaded SetUIEquipmentCmd contract; `equipment on/off` routes VSP pumps through SetPumpSpeed with speed=100 (on) or 0 (off).
reason: Root cause of every 'Input string was not in a correct format' (Hayward .NET status 6) on VSP pump operations. Side-by-side packet capture against the canonical Python wrapper (djtimca/omnilogic-api 0.6.1) showed Hayward overloads IsOn on SetUIEquipmentCmd: dataType=int with 0-100 sets a VSP speed; dataType=bool with True/False toggles non-VSP equipment. There is NO separate Speed param — the previous Go code sent BOTH IsOn=bool AND Speed=int, which Hayward's handler rejected. Even the Python wrapper's set_equipment(isOn=bool) fails against a VSP (Hayward quirk, not a Go bug). Fix: SetPumpSpeed drops Speed, sends IsOn=int; SetEquipment documented as non-VSP-only; IsVSPPump(cfg, equipmentID) detects VSPs by MSP-config type prefix (FMT_VARIABLE_SPEED / PMP_VARIABLE_SPEED); equipment on/off routes VSPs through SetPumpSpeed with DefaultVSPOnSpeed (100) for on and 0 for off.
validated_outcome: Live-verified: pump set-speed 'Filter Pump' --bow Pool --speed 50 returns status:ok and telemetry confirms filterSpeed=50. equipment on/off rounds-trip to speed 100/0. TestIsVSPPump and TestSetPumpSpeed_IsOnAsInt lock the detection + envelope shape.
